title: Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare: A Review of Applications
authors: A. S. L. Amin, M. A. Kabir
abstract: This paper reviews the role of artificial intelligence in healthcare. AI has shown promising capabilities in medical diagnostics, digital health records, and clinical prediction systems.

title: Deep Learning for Healthcare: Systematic Review of Applications
authors: R. Miotto, F. Wang, S. Wang, X. Jiang
abstract: A comprehensive review of deep learning techniques applied to electronic health records, clinical imaging, and physiological signals to enhance diagnostic accuracy.

title: Clinical Applications of Artificial Intelligence in Hospital Workflows
abstract: This paper investigates how clinical workflows can be optimized using automated machine learning scoring models in hospital emergency departments.

title: Machine Learning for Patient Care: An Evaluation of Clinical Trials
abstract: We evaluate the impact of deploying machine learning recommendation engines for clinical decisions across multi-center clinical trials.

title: Digital Health Systems and Artificial Intelligence Integration
abstract: This study analyzes the integration of artificial intelligence in digital health platforms, highlighting interoperability and data pipeline challenges.

title: AI in Healthcare Systems: Ethical and Regulatory Perspectives
abstract: Ethical and legal considerations surrounding the use of machine learning models in critical diagnostic software in healthcare platforms.

title: Healthcare Automation via Machine Learning and Decision Trees
abstract: We present an automated decision support system using robust gradient boosting models to classify patient severity scores.

title: A Survey on Healthcare AI Architectures and Security Protocols
abstract: A security review of cloud-based medical data processing systems that run deep convolutional networks on edge nodes.

title: Deep Learning Models for Health Informatics and Patient Outcome Prediction
abstract: We present a deep recurrent neural network designed to predict length of stay and mortality using raw medical telemetry logs.

title: Machine Learning for Patient Care: Abstraction and Real-world Deployment
abstract: An empirical study of deployment strategies for patient monitoring models, assessing accuracy drift over a 12-month window.

title: Deep Learning for ECG Analysis: A Systematic Survey
abstract: A survey of deep learning algorithms applied to electrocardiogram analysis, focusing on classification of arrhythmias and feature extraction.

title: Cardiovascular Risk Prediction using Self-Supervised ECG Representations
abstract: We demonstrate that self-supervised pre-training on large unlabeled ECG datasets yields high-fidelity representations for cardiovascular risk scoring.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Cardiovascular Medicine: Clinical Applications
abstract: An overview of how deep learning architectures assist cardiologists in detecting heart failure, ischemic heart disease, and coronary risk from patient data.

title: ECG Anomaly Detection using Recurrent Attention Networks
abstract: This paper presents a recurrent model equipped with attention layers to identify temporal anomalies in continuous multi-lead ECG signals.

title: Deep Learning for Cardiovascular Risk Scoring in Large Populations
abstract: We develop a convolutional network to estimate ten-year cardiovascular risk scores from non-invasive clinical biometric measurements.

title: ECG Classification using Multi-Scale Convolutional Neural Networks
abstract: A multi-scale CNN architecture designed to capture both local waveform changes and global rhythm variations in electrocardiograms.

title: AI and Cardiology: Challenges in Clinical Translation
abstract: We review key bottlenecks preventing the clinical integration of AI models in cardiology workflows, highlighting interpretability and validation.

title: Cardiovascular Risk Assessment using Machine Learning Classifiers
abstract: A comparison of gradient boosting machines, random forests, and support vector machines for prediction of acute coronary syndromes.

title: ECG Analysis using Foundation Models and Fine-Tuning
abstract: We adapt large transformer foundation models to model electrocardiograms, achieving competitive performance on lead classification tasks.

title: Cardiovascular Disease Prediction using Deep Neural Networks
abstract: A multi-layer neural network trained on electronic health record sequence patterns to forecast cardiovascular events in high-risk groups.

title: Deep Learning for Sepsis Prediction in Intensive Care Units
abstract: A recurrent neural network with LSTM cells is developed to predict the onset of sepsis in critical patients using multivariate time-series data.

title: Sepsis Detection in ICU using Time-Series Neural Networks
abstract: An early warning model for sepsis using temporal convolutional networks that achieves early prediction up to six hours prior to clinical onset.

title: AI Models for Early Detection of Sepsis in Clinical Settings
abstract: A validation study assessing a real-time sepsis risk calculator deployed in a major clinical intensive care unit, detailing positive predictive values.

title: Real-Time Sepsis Prediction using Multi-Task Deep Learning

title: Deep Learning for Sepsis Detection and Risk Stratification
abstract: A deep classifier designed to identify distinct physiological sub-phenotypes of sepsis from continuous patient vitals data.

title: Early Sepsis Detection using Gradient Boosted Decision Trees
abstract: A lightweight gradient boosted tree classifier for clinical sepsis prediction, optimized for low latency in resource-constrained clinics.

title: Sepsis Prediction Models: A Systematic Performance Analysis

title: AI in ICU Sepsis Management: Clinical and Algorithmic Challenges
abstract: We outline algorithms for sepsis risk forecasting, explaining technical constraints such as missing value imputation in EHR databases.

title: Explainable AI for Sepsis Risk Scoring in Emergency Settings

title: Early Detection of Sepsis using Sequence-to-Sequence Models

title: Deep Learning for Drug-Target Interaction Prediction

title: Generative Models for De Novo Drug Design and Lead Generation

title: Explainable AI for Patient Risk Scoring and Prediction

title: SHAP and LIME in Medical AI: Comparison of Clinical Explanations

title: Explainable Deep Learning for Clinical EEG Analysis

title: Clinical Evaluations of XAI Systems: A Physician Study

title: Federated Learning for Brain Tumor Segmentation: Multi-Institutional

title: Federated Learning in Multi-Institutional Studies: A Clinical Review

title: Federated Learning for Electronic Health Records Prediction

title: Secure Federated Learning in Medicine: A Cryptographic Approach

title: Federated Clinical Decision Support: Methods and Evaluations
